Class GameEngine

java.lang.Object
GameEngine
public class GameEngine extends Object
GameEngine class - handles the game logic of the text adventure "Henriette's Feast". It processes commands, manages the current room and movement history, and interacts with the user interface.
Version:
2026.05.09
Author:
Michael Kolling and David J. Barnes + D. Bureau + Raphaël Quillaud

  • Field Details

    • aPlayer

      private Player aPlayer

    • aParser

  • Constructor Details

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine()
      Constructor for GameEngine. Initializes the rooms, parser, and movement history.

  • Method Details

    • createRooms

      private void createRooms()
      Creates all rooms of the village and connects them with exits. Also sets the starting room (Henriette's kitchen).

    • goRoom

      private void goRoom(Command pCommand)
      Tries to go to the room in the direction given by the command. If there is no second word or no exit in that direction, prints an error message.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the direction

    • look

      private void look()
      Executes the 'look' command. Prints the current room description and the items present in the room.

    • eat

      private void eat(Command pCommand)
      Executes the 'eat' command. If the player eats a cookie, their maximum weight capacity is doubled. Other items cannot be eaten.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the name of the item to eat

    • back

      private void back()
      Moves the player back to the previous room, if available. If there are no previous rooms in the navigation history, a message is displayed indicating that the action is not possible. Otherwise, the last visited room is retrieved from the stack and set as the current room, and the location information is updated.

    • take

      private void take(Command pCommand)
      Picks up an item from the current room and adds it to the player's inventory.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the name of the item to take

    • drop

      private void drop(Command pCommand)
      Drops the item currently carried by the player into the current room.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the name of the item to drop

    • charge

      private void charge(Command pCommand)
      Charges the beamer with the current room.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the beamer name

    • fire

      private void fire(Command pCommand)
      Fires the beamer, teleporting the player to the charged room.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the beamer name

    • test

      private void test(Command pCommand)
      Tests the commands provided in a specified file by interpreting and executing them sequentially. The file should contain one command per line. Commands are ignored if the file is not found, or if the command cannot be interpreted. If the command does not have a second word specifying the file name, a usage message is displayed. If the specified file cannot be located, an error message is displayed.
      Parameters:
      pCommand - the command containing the file name as the second word

    • printWelcome

      private void printWelcome()
      Prints the welcome text and the initial location information.

    • printLocationInfo

      private void printLocationInfo()
      Prints the current room description and its available exits.

    • printHelp

      private void printHelp()
      Prints a help message describing the game objective and the available commands.

    • interpretCommand

      public void interpretCommand(String pCommandLine)
      Interprets and executes the given command line.
      Parameters:
      pCommandLine - the command line entered by the user

    • setGUI

      public void setGUI(UserInterface pUserInterface)
      Stores the graphical user interface and prints the welcome message.
      Parameters:
      pUserInterface - the graphical user interface of the game

    • endGame

      private void endGame()
      Ends the game by printing a goodbye message and disabling input.
